name: CyberArk Privilege Cloud
description: SaaS privileged access management on CyberArk Identity Security Platform Shared Services. Yields safe and privileged account inventories, session recording metadata, access policy reports and audit activity evidence. Recipes target Privilege Cloud (SaaS); self-hosted PAM console paths (PVWA) differ.

L1.title: CyberArk Privilege Cloud Manual Evidence Collection

steps.action: Sign in to the Privilege Cloud portal via the CyberArk Identity Security Platform and navigate to Reports (self-hosted PAM users: the equivalent is the PVWA REPORTS page)
steps.security_note: Use a dedicated auditor account with MFA for evidence collection, never a day-to-day Privilege Cloud Administrators account

steps.action: Generate the Privileged Accounts Inventory report and export it as CSV or Excel to evidence the safe and account inventory
steps.audit_note: Reports only contain data the generating user is authorised to see — run them with auditor-level access so the inventory is complete

steps.action: Open the Sessions monitoring page, filter recorded privileged sessions to the collection period, and capture the recordings grid (user, target account, start time, duration) as a dated screenshot or export
steps.permissions_required: Authorised auditor with access to the PSMRecordings Safe
steps.security_note: Session recordings can expose target hostnames and on-screen data — collect recording metadata as evidence, not the video files, unless a control specifically requires them
steps.audit_note: Recordings are stored in the PSMRecordings Safe and are tamper-resistant, which strengthens their evidentiary value

steps.action: Open Audit in the Identity Security Platform, filter activities to the collection period, and download the audit events to CSV
steps.audit_note: Audit events are timestamped by CyberArk; keep collection periods contiguous so there are no gaps between exports

steps.action: Upload the exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: PAM exports enumerate every privileged account in the estate — treat them as highly sensitive, restrict repository access, and confirm no credential values are present before uploading

L2.title: CyberArk Privilege Cloud Scheduled Reports Collection

steps.action: In the Privilege Cloud portal, navigate to Reports and create the reports you need for evidence — for example Privileged Accounts Inventory and Privileged Accounts Compliance Status
steps.permissions_required: Privilege Cloud Administrators role (or a dedicated reports role assigned to a reporting group)

steps.action: Set each report to generate automatically on a weekly or monthly schedule matching your evidence collection cadence
steps.audit_note: Scheduled generation removes reliance on individuals remembering to run reports, and each report carries a CyberArk generation timestamp

steps.action: Confirm generated reports are stored in the PVWAReports Safe and restrict Safe membership to the compliance team and the collection service account
steps.permissions_required: Privilege Cloud Administrators role to manage Safe membership
steps.security_note: Scheduled inventory reports list all privileged accounts — keep PVWAReports Safe membership to the minimum set of authorised users

steps.action: Add scheduled Entitlement and Activity reports to evidence access policies (who can access what) and privileged activity over time
steps.audit_note: Entitlement reports evidence access policy state; Activity reports evidence actual usage — collect both for access review controls

steps.action: Each cycle, retrieve the generated reports from the PVWAReports Safe and file them in the evidence repository, recording the generation timestamp against the evidence item
steps.audit_note: Verify a report was produced for every scheduled period; a missing period is a collection gap that should be investigated and documented

L3.title: CyberArk Privilege Cloud API-Driven Automated Collection

steps.action: In the Identity Administration portal, create a dedicated service user with the 'Is OAuth confidential client' option selected, and assign it to the Privilege Cloud Auditors role for read-only collection
steps.permissions_required: Identity Administration rights to create service users and manage role membership
steps.security_note: This client can read your entire privileged account estate — scope it to auditor-level access only, store the client secret in a secrets manager, and rotate it on a defined schedule
steps.audit_note: Service user creation and role assignment are themselves audited events — retain them as evidence of controlled credential issuance

steps.action: Authenticate by calling POST https://<tenant-id>.id.cyberark.cloud/oauth2/platformtoken with grant_type=client_credentials to obtain a bearer token for Shared Services APIs
steps.security_note: Tokens are short-lived — request a fresh token per collection run and hold it in memory only; never write tokens to disk or logs

steps.action: Call GET https://<subdomain>.privilegecloud.cyberark.cloud/PasswordVault/API/Accounts to export the privileged account inventory, paging through all results
steps.permissions_required: List Accounts permission in each Safe, granted via Safe membership for the service user
steps.audit_note: The API returns only accounts in Safes the service user can list — reconcile the count against the Privileged Accounts Inventory report to confirm completeness

steps.action: Call GET https://<subdomain>.privilegecloud.cyberark.cloud/PasswordVault/API/Recordings with date filters to collect privileged session recording metadata for the period
steps.permissions_required: Auditor access to the PSMRecordings Safe
steps.security_note: Collect recording metadata only (session properties, timings, targets); do not bulk-download recording media through the pipeline

steps.action: Schedule the collection script daily via cron or a CI/CD pipeline, writing dated JSON/CSV outputs to the evidence repository, with error handling that alerts the compliance team on any failed run
steps.security_note: Run the job from a hardened host; API throttling in Privilege Cloud can reject bursts, so implement retry with backoff rather than tight retry loops

steps.action: Validate each run: compare account and session counts against the previous run's baseline and flag unexplained drops for investigation
steps.audit_note: Volume validation demonstrates collection completeness and turns the pipeline itself into auditable evidence

L4.title: CyberArk Privilege Cloud SIEM-Integrated Evidence Pipeline

steps.action: Configure the Audit service's agentless SIEM integration to stream Privilege Cloud audit events to your SIEM over syslog (TCP or TLS only — UDP is not supported; up to five syslog targets)
steps.security_note: Use TLS for the syslog transport; the agentless integration requires no on-premises agent or Secure Tunnel component

steps.action: Enable forwarding for all evidence-relevant event categories: Vault Audit (credential access), Session Audit, Safe Management, User Management and Policy Changes
steps.audit_note: Streaming all five categories gives a complete, continuously collected audit picture of privileged activity, access policy changes and account lifecycle events

steps.action: Track collection completeness metrics in the SIEM: events per day per category, ingestion latency and coverage across all Safes and platforms
steps.audit_note: Completeness metrics are the key evidence that the collection process itself is managed and monitored

steps.action: Reconcile the event stream periodically against daily API pulls of the account inventory (GET /PasswordVault/API/Accounts) to detect silent streaming gaps
steps.audit_note: Cross-source reconciliation catches gaps that single-source monitoring misses — document each reconciliation as evidence

steps.action: Build a collection health dashboard (events/day, latency, per-category coverage, failed deliveries) and alert the compliance team when volume drops below baseline or a syslog target stops receiving
steps.security_note: A stalled privileged-activity feed is a security incident as well as a compliance gap — route gap alerts to security operations, not just compliance

steps.action: Run an automated quarterly review that produces a collection health report, flags any SLA breaches or gaps, and files the report in the evidence repository
